steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that European panda fossils span over 10 million years, and the Bulgarian teeth are described as 'relatively recent' among them. The question asks what the discovery most logically implies about the origin debate.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the teeth suggest pandas originated in Europe': A more recent European fossil cannot establish where pandas first appeared — it only documents later survival. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the teeth confirm a European origin over an Asian one': Same problem — recency of evidence cannot determine earlier origins. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the teeth push back the European fossil record': The teeth are relatively recent, so they do not push the record earlier. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the teeth cannot resolve where pandas originated': A younger fossil only shows pandas were still present later; it adds nothing to the origin question. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
